research.interface: Terraform Enterprise REST API v2 (/api/v2, JSON:API): configuration-versions and runs endpoints (stable)

official_sources.title: API documentation for Terraform Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: /api/v2 base path, Authorization: Bearer token auth, application/vnd.api+json JSON:API media type, JSON:API errors array shape, 429 rate limiting, and 404 responses masking unauthorized resources

official_sources.title: /configuration-versions API reference for Terraform Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: POST /workspaces/:workspace_id/configuration-versions body (type configuration-versions, auto-queue-runs attribute), upload-url attribute, PUT of the raw archive with Content-Type application/octet-stream to the pre-signed URL with no Authorization header, and status values pending/fetching/uploaded/archived/errored

official_sources.title: /runs API reference for Terraform Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: POST /runs body with data.type runs, attributes.message, and workspace/configuration-version relationships; the run status state machine (planned, planned_and_finished, applied, errored, discarded, canceled, force_canceled, policy_checked, policy_soft_failed, policy_override, ...); actions.is-confirmable; and POST /runs/:run_id/actions/apply with an optional {"comment"} body returning 202

research.language_rationale: HashiCorp's official Go SDK is go-tfe, but this harness forbids third-party modules; the documented raw JSON:API REST surface at /api/v2 is the canonical, fully documented integration contract (go-tfe is a thin client over exactly these endpoints), and Go's stdlib net/http plus encoding/json is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to speak it.

verified_facts: All Terraform Enterprise API endpoints live under /api/v2 and require an Authorization: Bearer <token> header; request bodies use the JSON:API media type application/vnd.api+json.

A configuration version is created with POST /api/v2/workspaces/:workspace_id/configuration-versions with data.type configuration-versions; auto-queue-runs defaults to true, so a launcher that wants to control run creation must send auto-queue-runs: false.

The create response includes an upload-url attribute — a temporary pre-signed archivist URL; the archive is uploaded by PUTting the raw tar.gz bytes to it with Content-Type: application/octet-stream and no Authorization header.

A configuration version can only be used by a run once its status is uploaded; documented states include pending, fetching, uploaded, archived, and errored, and clients poll GET /api/v2/configuration-versions/:id to observe the transition.

A run is created with POST /api/v2/runs whose data carries type runs, an attributes.message, and JSON:API relationships to the workspace (type workspaces) and configuration-version (type configuration-versions).

Run status values include pending, plan_queued, planning, planned, cost_estimating, cost_estimated, policy_checking, policy_checked, policy_override, policy_soft_failed, confirmed, apply_queued, applying, applied, planned_and_finished, errored, discarded, canceled, and force_canceled; data.attributes.actions.is-confirmable reports whether the run awaits confirmation.
A run is confirmed with POST /api/v2/runs/:run_id/actions/apply with an optional {"comment": ...} JSON body; success is HTTP 202 with no meaningful body.

Errors are JSON:API error documents — an errors array of objects with status, title, and detail — and Terraform Enterprise deliberately answers 404 both for missing resources and for resources the caller is not authorized to access.
The API rate-limits at up to 30 requests per second, answering 429 with a JSON:API error document when exceeded.
